current_conditions.site_name: ETOWAH RIVER AT GA 9, NEAR DAWSONVILLE, GA

water_temp_read.read: Water is very warm for trout.

light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

caution_notes: Warm water may increase trout stress.

generated_text.quick_read: Trout fishing on the Etowah River is currently tough due to very warm water temperatures. Recent stocking provides some opportunity using attractor and stocked-trout flies.
generated_text.best_window: Day 1 morning is best because recent stocking supports active feeding on egg patterns and attractor nymphs, creating the highest confidence for trout activity despite warm water conditions.
generated_text.current_conditions: USGS reports water temperature at 81°F, flow at 98.9 cfs, gage height 3.7 ft, and turbidity 6.6 NTU, indicating warm and moderately clear water conditions.
generated_text.weather_setup: Sky cover averages 20% with a pressure of 30.04 inHg showing no strong trend. Wind is from the west at 4.7 mph with gusts up to 16.1 mph. Rain chances are low with 0 inches expected.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will seek cold-water refuges and oxygenated runs, seams, and structure. Use clean drifts with smaller natural and stocked trout attractor patterns like egg patterns, squirmy worms, and Duracells. Warm water may stress trout, so consider not targeting them aggressively.
